title: Mercer Superannuation (Australia) Limited — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court ordered Mercer Superannuation (Australia) Limited to pay an $11.3 million penalty — ASIC's first successful greenwashing civil penalty case — after Mercer admitted to making misleading statements that seven 'Sustainable Plus' superannuation investment options excluded investments in carbon-intensive industries such as thermal coal, when adequate systems were not in place to ensure the ESG claims were accurate or that exclusions were enforced.
scope: Federal Court civil penalty of $11.3 million for greenwashing conduct, admitted by the company; Court also made an adverse publicity order requiring disclosure of the contraventions; Root cause found to be inadequate systems to verify and enforce ESG exclusion claims
